RT @winngamerx: Anthropic IPO Closing Market Cap around <$1.25T

this is my current base case for when Anthropic eventually goes public

wh…

Anthropic IPO Closing Market Cap around <$1.25T

this is my current base case for when Anthropic eventually goes public

why:
> Anthropic had already confidentially filed paperwork at a ~$965B valuation with ARR reportedly reaching $47B

> the company has very strong enterprise traction and high gross margins, which supports a premium valuation

> however, the recent Fable 5 regulatory drama and uncertainty around model access have created some investor caution

> Anthropic is growing extremely fast, but it still trails OpenAI significantly in both revenue and overall market perception

comparison to OpenAI (GPT):
> Anthropics IPO market cap will most likely land at 45–55% of OpenAIs valuation at the time of its IPO

do you think Anthropic can close the valuation gap with OpenAI before IPO?

RT @winngamerx: Next Google Gemini Pro Model released by July 15

i think this is the most probable timeline

why:
> at Google I/O 2026 in…

Next Google Gemini Pro Model released by July 15

i think this is the most probable timeline

why:
> at Google I/O 2026 in May, Google announced the Gemini 3.5 series and explicitly said Gemini 3.5 Pro would reach general availability "this month" (referring to June)

> Google has been following a pattern of releasing the Flash version first, then quickly following up with the stronger Pro version in the same family

> competitive pressure is high right now. With Claude Fable 5 drama and OpenAI expected to drop GPT-5.6 very soon, Google has strong incentive to ship Gemini 3.5 Pro without delay

> internal testing and early access for Gemini 3.5 Pro have already been reported in multiple sources since mid-June

for context:
> Gemini 3.5 Pro is expected to be a meaningful step up from Gemini 3.1 Pro, particularly in reasoning, long-context handling, and agentic capabilities, while keeping Googles traditional advantage in speed and price-to-performance

> it will likely become the new default high-end model for many users and enterprise customers

IMPORTANT:
> i would advise using limit orders in this market, as trading liquidity is currently very low!

do you think Google will release the next Gemini Pro model before mid-July?

Next Claude Sonnet released by August 10

I think this is the most probable window

why:
> Anthropic has been releasing Sonnet updates on a fairly consistent cadence

> the last one (Sonnet 4.6) came out in mid-February, so were now well into the typical timeframe for the next version

> after shipping Fable 5 in early June (even though it got suspended), Anthropic will likely want to refresh the Sonnet line to keep momentum and give users a strong, always-available model while the Fable situation is being resolved

> Sonnet models are usually released relatively quickly after major Opus or new-tier updates

> with Opus 4.8 already out since late May, a new Sonnet is overdue

> Anthropic needs a competitive daily driver to counter GPT-5.5 / upcoming GPT-5.6 and Gemini updates

for context:  
> the next Sonnet (likely Sonnet 5 or a high 4.x version) will probably focus on better speed, improved agentic performance, and possibly a more stable 1M+ context experience

> it wont be as big a leap as Fable 5, but it will be the model most people actually use day-to-day once it drops

do you think the next Claude Sonnet will arrive before mid-August?

GPT-5.6 released by July 10

I think this is the most probable timeline

why:
> OpenAI has been on a roughly 6-week release cadence with the GPT-5.x series (GPT-5.4 in early March, GPT-5.5 in late April)

> chief scientist Jakub Pachocki has already described GPT-5.6 internally as a "meaningful improvement" over GPT-5.5, with focus on agentic performance and efficiency

> the model has already appeared in OpenAIs internal Codex routing logs, which usually happens close to release

for context:
> GPT-5.6 is not expected to be a massive generational leap like GPT-5 was

> its more of a strong iterative improvement and better agentic workflows, improved token efficiency, and likely a larger context window

> its OpenAIs direct response to Claude Opus 4.8 and the brief appearance of Fable 5

do you think GPT-5.6 will be released within the next two weeks?

RT @winngamerx: Anthropic will be the best AI model again by end of July

I believe this is the most likely outcome

why:
> Fable 5 restore…

Anthropic will be the best AI model again by end of July

I believe this is the most likely outcome

why:
> Fable 5 restored for US customers by mid-July would immediately put Anthropic back in first place, as it was clearly ahead of GPT-5.5 during its short time online

> even without full global access, Claude Opus 4.8 is already the strongest publicly available model today across coding, agentic work, and most frontier benchmarks

> anthropic has every incentive to restore Fable 5 quickly and its their most powerful model and they are actively fighting the restrictions

> openAIs GPT-5.5 is very close, but currently trails in the areas that matter most for complex, long-running tasks

so should i buy Anthropic at 85%?

Fable 5 restored for US customers by July 17

why:
> anthropic is highly motivated to bring back its strongest model

> there is a lawsuit from a client against the us government

> administration officials (specifically the ai advisor) have publicly stated they want to fix everything quickly if anthropic "fixes the safety issue"

> technically, anthropic can relatively quickly enable access only for us users (via verification/id) while the issue with foreigners is being resolved

information for non-us customers:
> it will most likely happen later, when the export ban is either lifted/relaxed or anthropic creates a separate restricted mode

> a full global return might take longer

do you think this will happen within the next month?

Senegal vs Iraq

why Senegal won:
> they were more clinical in front of goal and took their chances well
> showed better quality in the midfield and controlled the tempo of the game
> punished Iraq’s defensive mistakes effectively throughout the match

why Iraq lost:
> made costly errors in defense that Senegal capitalized on
> struggled to keep possession and often gave the ball away in dangerous areas
> lacked enough attacking threat to really trouble Senegal

do you think Iraq could have made it more difficult for Senegal if they didnt make those defensive mistakes?

Norway vs France

why France won:
> they were more clinical in front of goal and took their chances efficiently
> showed better quality in the midfield and controlled the tempo of the game
> punished Norway’s defensive mistakes and created more dangerous moments

why Norway lost:
> made costly errors in defense that France capitalized on
> struggled to keep possession and often gave the ball away in dangerous areas
> lacked enough attacking threat to really trouble France throughout the match

do you think Norway could have made it more difficult for France if they didnt make those defensive mistakes?

Morocco 4-2 Haiti

why Morocco won:
> they were more clinical in front of goal and took their chances efficiently
> showed better quality in attack and created more dangerous moments throughout the game
> managed to control the game after going ahead and didn’t let Haiti come back

why Haiti lost:
> made several defensive mistakes that Morocco punished quickly
> struggled to keep possession and often gave the ball away in dangerous areas
> their defense looked disorganized, especially in the second half

market volume:
> $54.10m

do you think Haiti could have made it more difficult for Morocco if they didn’t make those defensive errors?

Czechia 0-3 Mexico

why Mexico won:
> they were much more clinical in front of goal and took their chances well
> dominated the midfield and controlled the tempo of the game
> punished Czechia’s defensive mistakes and played with high intensity

why Czechia lost:
> made several costly errors in defense that Mexico capitalized on
> struggled to keep possession and were constantly under pressure
> lacked attacking quality and couldn’t create enough danger against Mexico

market volume:
> $25.48m

do you think Mexicos home support played a big role in how comfortably they won this game?

Portugal 5-0 Uzbekistan

why Portugal won:
> they were extremely clinical in front of goal and converted almost every chance they created
> dominated possession and controlled the tempo of the game from start to finish
> showed excellent attacking movement and constantly created problems for Uzbekistans defense
> RONALDOOOOO!!!!!

why Uzbekistan lost:
> their defense was very disorganized and made multiple costly mistakes
> struggled to keep possession and were constantly under pressure
> lacked any real attacking threat and couldn’t create danger on the counter-attack
> NO RONALDO

do you think Portugal were just that much better or Uzbekistan made it way too easy for them?

England 0-0 Ghana

why England didnt win:
> they created chances but lacked quality and sharpness in the final third
> played too slow and predictable against Ghana’s compact and well-organized defense
> didn’t show enough urgency or creativity when it was needed most

why Ghana didnt win:
> lacked attacking quality and rarely created dangerous moments on the counter
> struggled to keep possession and build proper attacks against England
> were mostly focused on defending and didn’t have enough threat going forward

do you think England were too comfortable and didnt push hard enough to win this game?

Panama 0-1 Croatia

why Croatia won:
> they were more clinical in front of goal and took their only real chance well
> controlled the game better in the second half and limited Panama’s threats
> showed good composure and didn’t panic when Panama tried to push forward

why Panama lost:
> made a costly defensive mistake that Croatia punished immediately
> struggled to create clear chances despite having some possession
> lacked the quality in the final third to really trouble Croatia’s defense

do you think Panama could’ve gotten a point if they didn’t make that defensive error?

Colombia 1-0 DR Cong

why Colombia won:
> they were more clinical in front of goal and created better quality chances
> controlled the midfield and limited DR Congo’s counter-attacks effectively
> showed better organization and made fewer mistakes than their opponents

why DR Congo lost:
> struggled to keep possession and often gave the ball away in dangerous areas
> their defense looked disorganized, especially when Colombia increased the pressure
> lacked enough attacking threat to really trouble Colombia throughout the match

do you think DR Congo could have made it harder for Colombia if they were more clinical on the counter?
